findings.title: Changing language from Settings leaves the page's own chrome in the old language (partial repaint)
findings.rootCause: setLocale() (i18n.ts:157) writes STATE.preferences.locale + emit('state:changed') but never re-mounts or re-navigates. In Settings.tsx the top-level Settings() component subscribes ONLY to the tab store (useSettingsTabSnapshot), and MenuView()/SubTabStrip() subscribe to no store at all. React only re-renders a component when one of ITS OWN hooks fires, so only the store-subscribed sub-sections (GeneralLanguageSection/GeneralAppearanceSection via useStore) repaint on a locale change; the surrounding chrome that renders t() strings does not. paintI18nBindings (main.ts:178) only repaints legacy [data-i18n-key] DOM nodes, not React text. The Personalization page does NOT have this bug because Personalization() calls useStore at its top, so its whole subtree repaints.

findings.title: Sessions and Blocked-users settings panels are entirely hardcoded English (no i18n)
findings.rootCause: Both panels were shipped with the translation pass deliberately skipped. Sessions.tsx admits it in a comment (lines 118-120: 'keys for these strings can be added … for now we hard-code English'); _deviceLabel/_formatRelativeTime, the confirm messages (lines 93-98), the headings (123-127), the 'This device' chip (168), and the button labels (200) are literals. Blocks.tsx uses only 4 t() calls and otherwise hard-codes its strings. The verified locale tables (en/es/fr/pt) are key-complete (1540 keys each, 0 missing), so these surfaces simply never route through t().

findings.title: Revoked creator can no longer delete their own Trip Templates (delete gated on is_creator before ownership)
findings.rootCause: delete_template (templates.py:482) checks `if not _is_creator(cursor, user_id): return 403` (line 486) BEFORE running the ownership-scoped `DELETE … WHERE id=? AND owner_id=?` (line 488). Deleting your OWN content should require ownership, not the (separately revocable) can-CREATE privilege. set_creator (admin.py) only flips the flag and intentionally leaves templates live, but combined with this gate the user is locked out of managing their own published content after revocation.

findings.title: Factory Reset wipes data but silently re-logs the user in (HttpOnly session cookie survives)
findings.rootCause: confirmResetApp (Settings.tsx:186) DELETEs /api/user-data, clears local STATE (including STATE.user = null, line 210), then does localStorage.clear() + location.reload() (lines 214-215). The session JWT lives in an HttpOnly `gg_session` cookie that JS cannot clear and that only /api/auth/logout invalidates (api/core.ts:57). location.reload() therefore re-runs restoreSession(), which succeeds on the surviving cookie, so the user is re-authenticated. The server data is genuinely wiped (so this is not data corruption), but the behavior contradicts the 'Erase Everything' framing and the explicit STATE.user=null.

findings.title: Edit-Category modal mixes localized and hardcoded-English strings
findings.rootCause: openEditCategoryModal (settings.ts:95) builds the modal via showModal innerHTML with hardcoded literals: the <h2> 'Edit Category' (line 106), the input placeholder='Category name' (line 110), and the Cancel button text (line 115). Only the submit button uses t('settings.editCategorySaveBtn') (line 114). This legacy helper was left in settings.ts during the React migration and never fully i18n'd.

findings.title: Theme and POI customization are device-local while Language syncs across devices, despite identical Settings presentation
findings.rootCause: STATE.preferences (theme, mapDefaultPois, poiFilters, poiVisible, poiAnchoring, pillEpicenters, enabledPois) is persisted ONLY to localStorage (state.ts:59 'Stored on this device only'; subscribe(STATE_CHANGED, saveState) at state.ts:438). The server has no preferences column. Language is special-cased: setLocale (i18n.ts:192) additionally POSTs /api/profile/update {language}, stored in users.language and hydrated into STATE.preferences.locale on boot (main.ts:123-129). So locale is the only cross-device preference; theme/POI silently are not. The theme/POI copy doesn't promise sync, so this is an inconsistency rather than a broken promise, but the parallel UI invites the wrong expectation.

findings.title: Admin dashboard: per-user trip count includes archived trips while global total excludes them
findings.rootCause: The global totalTrips (admin.py:136-141) counts only NON-archived trips (JOIN trip_members on the owner row WHERE COALESCE(is_archived,0)=0). The per-user roster's trip_count (admin.py:202) is `(SELECT COUNT(*) FROM trips t WHERE t.user_id = u.id)` — every owned trip regardless of archive state. The two definitions diverge for any user with archived trips.
